expressions.english: strange person
to taste weird
the weather is strange
the atmosphere is strange
You look pale.
expressions.notes:
얼굴색 = how healthy one looks
얼굴색이 이상하다 = to look pale

expressions.english: Don't judge me.
The weather has been strange lately.
Don't you think something's strange?
expressions.notes:
뭔가 = something (subject)
뭔가 is the contracted form of 무언가.

expressions.english: He has a really weird personality.
expressions.notes:
진짜 = really; very
성격 = personality, disposition

expressions.english: Stop talking about such strange things.
expressions.notes:
애기 = story; what one says
애기 is the contracted form of 이야기.
그만하다 = to stop, to quit

expressions.english: I feel strangely tired today.
expressions.notes:
오늘 = today
이상하게 is the adverb form of 이상하다.
피곤하다 = to be tired, exhausted; to be tiring

expressions.english: A weird person is following me.
expressions.notes:
절 = (polite) me
절 is the contracted form of 자를.
따라오다 = to follow (me)

expressions.english: I didn't feel good, so I went to see a doctor.
expressions.notes:
몸 = body
병원 = hospital
가다 = to go; to leave

expressions.english: This drama has a strange ending.
expressions.notes:
드라마 = TV drama, soap opera
결말 = a story's ending

expressions.english: Stop pulling such weird pranks.
expressions.notes:
자꾸 = repeatedly, again and again
농담 = prank, joke, trick

expressions.english: I had a really weird dream last night.
I followed the recipe, but it tastes weird.
expressions.notes:
조리법 = recipe
-대로 = according to; as suggested by
하다 = to do

expressions.english: Today, in particular, the atmosphere in the office is weird.
expressions.notes:
오늘따라 = today especially, today in particular
사무실 = office

expressions.korean: 이상해요. 여제 분명히 여기 났는데 없어요.
expressions.english: It's strange. I put it here, for sure, but it's not here.
expressions.notes:
분명히 = for sure, certainly, without doubt
여기 = here, this place
놓다 = to place, put; to let go

expressions.korean: 커피 맛이 이상해서 뭔더니 설탕 대신 소금을 넣은 거에요.
expressions.english: I wondered why the coffee tasted weird, and it turns out I had put in salt instead of sugar.
expressions.notes:
커피 = coffee
-왜/왜/뭔더니 = since/because + I/we + V-ed
설탕 = sugar
- 대신 = instead of + N/V-ing
소금 = salt
넣다 = to put (something) in
